params.description: Show the diff between last two commits

params.prompt:
Search this codebase thoroughly for:
1. All callers of `send()` method in adapters.py - where is timeout passed?
2. All places where timeout is set/configured by users (e.g. in sessions.py, api.py, models.py)
3. Test files that test timeout behavior
4. How `TimeoutSauce` is used elsewhere
5. The `send` method signature in BaseAdapter

Read relevant files. Report the key findings about timeout handling flow through the codebase.

final_text:
Here are my findings:

```json
[
  {
    "title": "User-specified timeout is silently discarded, all HTTP requests have no timeout",
    "category": "reliability",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 1.0,
    "file": "requests/adapters.py",
    "start_line": 471,
    "end_line": 472,
    "evidence": [
      "# Use no timeout - let connections take as long as they need",
      "timeout = None",
      "timeout=timeout,  # passed to conn.urlopen at line 485"
    ],
    "risk": "Every HTTP request made through the `requests` library will have no timeout regardless of what the user passes. This means any call to `requests.get(url, timeout=5)`, `session.request(url, timeout=(3.05, 27))`, etc. will block indefinitely if the server never responds or is slow. In production this causes threads/processes to hang forever, leading to resource exhaustion, thread pool starvation, cascading failures, and complete service outages. This is also a denial-of-service vector: any unresponsive upstream will permanently consume a thread/connection.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the original timeout conversion logic:\n\n```python\nif isinstance(timeout, tuple):\n    try:\n        connect, read = timeout\n        timeout = TimeoutSauce(connect=connect, read=read)\n    except ValueError:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Invalid timeout {timeout}. Pass a (connect, read) timeout tuple, \"\n            f\"or a single float to set both timeouts to the same value.\"\n        )\nelif isinstance(timeout, TimeoutSauce):\n    pass\nelse:\n    timeout = TimeoutSauce(connect=timeout, read=timeout)\n```"
  },
  {
    "title": "Tuple timeout validation removed — invalid timeout values (e.g., 3-tuples) silently ignored",
    "category": "correctness",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 1.0,
    "file": "requests/adapters.py",
    "start_line": 471,
    "end_line": 472,
    "evidence": [
      "Original code raised ValueError for invalid tuples: raise ValueError(f\"Invalid timeout {timeout}. Pass a (connect, read) timeout tuple, ...\")",
      "New code: timeout = None  # discards value without any validation"
    ],
    "risk": "Users passing malformed timeout values (e.g., a 3-tuple `(1, 2, 3)` or non-numeric types) will no longer receive a helpful ValueError. Instead, the value is silently discarded and replaced with None (no timeout). This masks programming errors in user code that previously would have been caught, making bugs harder to diagnose.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the original timeout validation logic that raises ValueError for invalid tuple formats."
  },
  {
    "title": "Security: removal of timeouts enables denial-of-service via unresponsive servers",
    "category": "security",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "file": "requests/adapters.py",
    "start_line": 471,
    "end_line": 472,
    "evidence": [
      "timeout = None  # hardcoded, ignoring user-supplied value",
      "timeout=timeout,  # None passed to urllib3's conn.urlopen()"
    ],
    "risk": "With timeout=None, a malicious or compromised upstream server can hold connections open indefinitely by trickling data or never completing the TCP handshake. An attacker who controls or can influence the target server (e.g., via SSRF or user-supplied URLs) can exhaust all available connections/threads in the client application, effectively causing a denial of service. Applications that previously protected themselves by setting explicit timeouts are now unprotected.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the original timeout handling that respects user-specified timeout values and converts them to urllib3 TimeoutSauce objects."
  }
]
```
